channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These AI-assisted innovations are from startups that are five years old or less
item.description: The Googles and Microsofts of the world hardly have a monopoly on innovation. Indeed, a number of recent tech breakthroughs have been delivered from organizations that aren’t even five years old.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: The 9 next big things in consumer technology for 2024
item.description: If you want to get excited about consumer tech again, look at these innovations. They include breakthroughs in material design and miniaturization to make your gadgets even sleeker; new technologies to improve your health and wellness; and some clever applications of AI and data analysis.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: The 4 next big things in fintech and crypto for 2024
item.description: It may have been a rocky year of ups and downs for fintechs and the blockchain as the American economy continues to give off strange vibes.
item.dc:creator: Connie Lin, Fast Company

item.title: Instagram rolls out option to reset recommendations
item.description: Instagram is testing a new feature that allows users to reset their content recommendations on the platform when in need of a "fresh start.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump expected to attend SpaceX launch on Tuesday
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk. The launch is scheduled for 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft and Atom Computing claim breakthrough in reliable quantum computing
item.description: Microsoft and Atom Computing have announced what they claim is a significant step forward in reliable quantum computing, unveiling a commercial quantum machine built with 24 entangled logical qubits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Madrid Investment Forum, escenario de los proyectos de innovación y atracción de talento del Ejecutivo regional
item.description: La Comunidad de Madrid continúa apostando por la consolidación de la capital como nodo de desarrollo tecnológico e industrial y polo de atracción de talento especializado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This tool is made for the laziest parent ever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how an innovative Easy-Way kit turns standard strollers electric, simplifying navigation for parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla Protest Camp Broken Up by German Police
item.description: German police break up protest camp near Tesla factory in Grünheide, citing public safety concerns and repeated violations of regulations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IFCO corta la cinta inaugural de su nuevo ‘hub’ digital global en Barcelona
item.description: La firma especializada en soluciones de envases reutilizables para alimentos frescos, IFCO, está de estreno; y es que recientemente ha cortado la cinta inaugural de su nuevo hub digital global en el Pier01 de Tech Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A GRC framework for securing generative AI
item.description: From automating workflows to unlocking new insights, generative AI models like OpenAI’s GPT-4 are already delivering value in enterprises across every industry.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Así es el ‘cambio radical’ de las TI en la búsqueda del éxito empresarial
item.description: La industria de los seguros, aunque tradicionalmente considerada sólida y estable, no está precisamente asociada con la toma de grandes riesgos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: A New Hampshire company is working to develop a fleet of autonomous helicopters that it hopes can be used to put out fires, spray crops and handle other dangerous jobs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: F# 9 adds nullable reference types
item.description: F# 9, the latest version of Microsoft’s open source functional language, has been released with support for nullable reference types and empty-bodied computation expressions. Standard library improvements also are featured, including random sampling functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden
item.description: OPM’s Dan Jacobs and stackArmor’s Gaurav “GP” Pal led an ACT-IAC team to develop a maturity model to help implement authority to operate as code. The post How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: PG&E blames birds for Tri-Valley power outages
item.description: According to the council’s agenda report filed ahead of Tuesday’s meeting, PG&E blamed birds for disrupting the region’s power supply.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Martin, The Mercury News

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: A federal judge in Delaware has dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: Randall Chase, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump team is seeking to ease US Rules for self-driving cars
item.description: Current federal rules pose significant roadblocks for companies looking to launch vehicles without steering wheels or pedals in large quantities, which Tesla intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: About 1 in 5 Americans say they get their news from social media influencers, according to a new poll from the Pew Research Center. Of the 21 percent of adults who said they get their news from influencers on social media, the split was roughly even across parties.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: IBM announces 50-fold quantum speed improvement
item.description: IBM launched its most advanced quantum computer yet last week at its inaugural quantum developer conference. It features nearly twice the gates of last year’s quantum utility demonstration – and a 50-fold speed increase.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Kyndryl, Microsoft expand mainframe modernization services
item.description: IT infrastructure services provider Kyndryl continues to build on its partnership with Microsoft, most recently adding new mainframe modernization support, security services, and cloud migration offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AI-Generated Mushroom Foraging Advice Sparks Concern
item.description: AI chatbot recommends cooking deadly mushroom, sparking concern among mushroom enthusiasts
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: T-Mobile hacked in massive Chinese cyber-espionage: Wall St. Journal
item.description: It was part of a campaign to spy on the phones of high-value intelligence targets, the Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AI, cybersecurity drive IT investments and lead skill shortages for 2025
item.description: AI and cybersecurity are driving enterprise tech investments and at the same time exposing the talent gaps that IT teams face.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: For November, Patch Tuesday includes three Windows zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft’s November Patch Tuesday release addresses 89 vulnerabilities in Windows, SQL Server, .NET and Microsoft Office — and three zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43451, CVE-2024-49019 and CVE-2024-49039) that mean a patch now recommendation for Windows platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Jen Psaki Warns of Disinformation Threat in 2024 US Presidential Election
item.description: Former White House press secretary Jen Psaki speaks out against disinformation threats in the 2024 US presidential election, emphasizing online safety and security measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: The EU seeks proposals for AI that should be banned
item.description: The EU, which is now developing guidelines for how the region’s new AI law must be complied with, has started collecting opinions in two areas via an online survey. The first area involves how the law should define AI systems (compared to traditional software).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Getting started with Google Password Manager
item.description: If you’re still trying to remember all of your passwords and then type ’em into sites by hand, let me tell you: You’re doing it wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11
